identity.mission: Create a 'Tactical Bureaucracy' interface: high-density, authoritative, and print-perfect.
principles: Density is Clarity: Maximize data per pixel without clutter.
Tactical Contrast: Use stark black/white with signal red for alerts.
Print First: The screen is just a preview of the paper.
Speed is Safety: Zero friction inputs for tired officers.

theme.visual_mood: White paper, crisp black ink, navy blue authority, alert red

headings.usage: Page titles, Section headers, Sheet labels

data.usage: Roster data, Star numbers, Dates, Times

scale.h2: text-xl font-bold tracking-tight text-slate-800 uppercase border-b-2 border-slate-900 pb-1
scale.label: text-[10px] font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest

usage_rules.alerts: Use 'bg-red-50 border-red-200 text-red-700' for duplicate officer warnings.
usage_rules.headers: Use 'bg-slate-100' for table headers to distinguish from data.
usage_rules.selection: Use 'bg-slate-900 text-white' for active tab states.
usage_rules.borders: All data grids must have 'border border-slate-300' for print clarity.

layout.print_styles: @media print { body { -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; } .no-print { display: none; } .page-break { page-break-before: always; } }

roster_sheet.description: The core spreadsheet component. Must be dense and printable.
specs: Use HTML <table> or CSS Grid for perfect alignment.
Sticky header for long lists (screen only).
Font: JetBrains Mono for all cell data.
Searchable by Name or Star Number.
Must show 'Seniority Date' in the dropdown list for context.
Simple list view with 'Add Officer' button top-right.
Background: Subtle police texture or clean slate.
Logo: 'OT ROSTER' text in Chivo Black.

images.description: Police hat on box - use with heavy overlay/blur for login background.

duplicate_detection.logic: If an officer ID is selected in more than one row across ANY active sheet (or same sheet), highlight BOTH rows in Red.
duplicate_detection.visual: Add class 'bg-red-100 text-red-900 border-red-500' to the row.

auto_populate.logic: On selection, immediately fill 'STAR', 'SEN DATE', and 'TIMESTAMP' columns.

sorting.logic: Dropdown list MUST be sorted by Seniority Date (Oldest date = Most Senior = Top of list).

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. FONTS: Add 'Chivo', 'Inter', and 'JetBrains Mono' via Google Fonts in index.html or CSS import.
4. STATE: Use a global state (Context or Zustand) to hold the 'Roster Data' so duplicate detection works across tabs.
5. PRINTING: Create a specific 'PrintView' component or use CSS media queries to hide sidebar/buttons when printing.
6. MOCK DATA: Create a robust 'mockOfficers' list with realistic names, star numbers, and seniority dates for testing.
8. DO NOT use 'framer-motion' for the grid itself (performance). Use it only for page transitions or modal entries.
universal_guidelines: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) sparingly (only for modals).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'.
You MUST NOT center align the app container.
Always use modern button styles: sharp corners ('rounded-sm') fit the tactical theme better than pill shapes.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable (except in the dense data grid).
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include 'data-testid' attribute.
